title: Static Frontend Hosting on Vercel
description: Host static frontends and JAMstack sites on Vercel with zero-config deployments, automatic HTTPS, per-branch preview environments, and serverless function support. First-class Next.js integration including ISR, Edge Middleware, and API routes.

cost.costNotes: Free tier (Hobby): 100GB bandwidth/month, 100GB-hours serverless function execution/month, unlimited deployments, automatic HTTPS, preview deployments for every PR. Pro plan ($20/month) for teams and increased limits.

provenance.estimatedMonthlyRangeUsd: This pattern explicitly targets Vercel's Hobby (free) tier, which provides 100GB bandwidth/month, 100GB-hours serverless function execution/month, unlimited personal deployments, and automatic HTTPS at $0/month. The min=0 and max=0 reflect that this pattern is specifically designed to remain within free tier limits. If usage exceeds free tier limits or team collaboration is required, users would transition to Vercel Pro ($20/month), but that represents a different pattern. This pattern is only applicable when traffic and function usage remain within Hobby tier boundaries.
provenance.adoptionCost: Low adoption cost due to Vercel's streamlined, zero-config deployment model and first-class Next.js support. Implementation involves: (1) Structuring application as JAMstack/Next.js architecture (1-2 hours for developers familiar with React/Next.js), (2) Configuring Git-based deployment via vercel.json or zero-config defaults (15-30 minutes), (3) Setting up serverless API routes if needed (1-2 hours), (4) Configuring environment variables and preview deployments (30 minutes). Total: 3-5 hours at $50/hour blended rate with AI coding assistants = $150-$250. AI tools like GitHub Copilot significantly reduce boilerplate for Next.js API routes and configuration. Vercel's zero-config defaults and tight Next.js integration reduce setup time compared to similar platforms.
provenance.licenseCost: Vercel's Hobby tier requires no license fees or upfront payments. The service operates on a freemium SaaS model where the Hobby tier is permanently available for personal and non-commercial projects. No commercial licenses, enterprise agreements, or per-seat costs for the free tier. Note: Vercel's free tier is intended for personal/hobby use; commercial projects require a Pro or Enterprise plan. The core hosting, serverless functions, and preview deployment features are genuinely free for qualifying projects.

provides.reasoning: Vercel's core offering is hosting static and server-side rendered sites, with particular optimization for Next.js applications.
Vercel Serverless Functions and Edge Functions allow backend logic execution without server management, essential for JAMstack backends.
Vercel includes a global edge network for content delivery with the Hobby tier's 100GB bandwidth, optimized for Next.js static and ISR content.
Vercel provides CI/CD pipeline capability with automatic deployments triggered by Git commits and preview deployments for every pull request.
Vercel's deployment model is fundamentally Git-based with GitHub/GitLab/Bitbucket integration, enabling continuous deployment from repository changes.
Vercel automatically creates preview deployments for every pull request, enabling teams to review changes before merging to production.

requires.reasoning: JAMstack architecture requires a tool to build static HTML/CSS/JS (e.g., Next.js, Vite, Astro) before deployment to Vercel.
Vercel's continuous deployment model requires source code to be in a Git repository for automatic builds and deployments.
JAMstack prototypes/MVPs typically need React/Vue/Svelte or similar framework; Vercel is especially optimized for Next.js (which Vercel created).

supports_nfr.reason: Hobby tier lacks enterprise SLA, multi-region redundancy guarantees, and priority support needed for high availability targets.
Vercel's edge network delivers fast static content, but serverless function cold starts introduce minimum p95 latency of 50ms.
Serverless function cold start variability and free-tier resource contention result in p99 latency floor of 150ms.
Vercel supports instant rollback to previous deployments via dashboard or CLI, enabling RTO as low as 1 minute.
Hobby tier bandwidth limit of 100GB/month and serverless function execution limits cap peak throughput to 36000 jobs/hour.
Hobby tier bandwidth and serverless function concurrency limits constrain read QPS to 100 maximum.
Hobby tier function execution limits cap write QPS at 50 maximum.
Vercel Hobby/Pro tiers do not offer a HIPAA BAA; PHI must not be processed or stored via Vercel serverless functions.

supports_constraints.reason: Vercel is a cloud-agnostic SaaS platform that abstracts underlying infrastructure (runs on AWS) from users.
Vercel supports static site generators and serverless functions in multiple languages, with first-class support for JavaScript and TypeScript (Next.js).
Vercel is specifically designed for web hosting, JAMstack applications, and API serverless functions.
Pattern specifically requires Vercel as the hosting provider.
Vercel edge network provides built-in CDN caching and Next.js ISR (Incremental Static Regeneration), but pattern works with or without application-level caching.
Multi-tenancy is supported at small scale with external free-tier databases, but not a pattern requirement.
Vercel serverless functions have execution time limits (10s Hobby tier) that preclude batch processing workloads.
Vercel serverless architecture and Hobby tier limitations do not support complex distributed transaction coordination.
Vercel Hobby tier does not include WebSocket or real-time streaming capabilities for live data.
Free tiers of external vector databases (Pinecone, Supabase) can be integrated for limited vector search in prototypes.
Vercel does not provide database services; document storage requires external services outside this pattern.
Vercel does not provide persistent key-value storage; requires external services like Redis Cloud or Upstash.
Vercel does not provide database services; graph database requirements need external providers.
Vercel is not designed for time-series data storage; requires external time-series database services.
Free-tier external databases can support OLTP workloads at small scale, but pattern works for both OLTP and non-OLTP.
Vercel is not designed for analytical processing workloads; optimized for static sites and lightweight serverless functions.

framework.description: Frontend framework. Next.js has first-class Vercel support (zero config, ISR, Edge Runtime). Remix and Astro offer strong SSR/SSG capabilities. Vite and SvelteKit are lighter alternatives.

function_runtime.description: Serverless function runtime. Node.js provides full Node.js API and broader npm ecosystem. Edge runtime executes at the CDN edge with faster cold starts but limited APIs (no fs, http modules).

enable_preview_deployments.description: Enable automatic preview deployments for every pull request. Provides unique URLs for each PR, enabling stakeholder review before merging. Recommended for team workflows.

edge_functions.description: Enable Edge Functions for middleware and low-latency compute at the CDN edge. Useful for A/B testing, authentication redirects, and geolocation-based routing. Adds complexity but reduces latency for eligible use cases.

warn_nfr.reason: At 99% availability, Vercel Hobby has no formal SLA — any SLO target above 99% (e.g., ops-slo-error-budgets defaulting to 99.9%) will be architecturally unachievable with this tier.
warn_nfr.message: hosting-static-frontend--vercel: availability target {actual} (99%) is the maximum supported by Hobby tier (no formal SLA). Any SLO target above 99% — e.g., ops-slo-error-budgets at 99.9% — burns error budget ~10× faster than the budget allows. Consider Vercel Pro for a contractual 99.99% SLA.

warn_nfr.reason: Hobby tier bandwidth limit of 100GB/month can be exhausted quickly at sustained high QPS, leading to service interruption.
warn_nfr.message: hosting-static-frontend--vercel: peak read QPS is {actual} req/s (>50). Hobby tier 100GB/month bandwidth may be exhausted quickly, causing service interruption mid-month.

warn_nfr.reason: Hobby tier serverless function execution limits (100GB-hours/month) can be exhausted at sustained high write QPS.
warn_nfr.message: hosting-static-frontend--vercel: peak write QPS is {actual} req/s (>25). Hobby tier function execution limits may be exhausted, causing throttling or service interruption.
